The '''DD-19 "Overseer" labor pool droid''', also known simply as the '''DD-19 "Overseer" Droid''' or '''DD-19 Overseer''', was a model of [[Supervisor droid/Legends|supervisor droid]] manufactured by [[Ubrikkian Steamworks]]. A DD-19 Overseer was among the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] infected by the [[Iron Eclipse]] [[Computer virus/Legends|technovirus]] created by [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco]] and [[Iaclyn Stark]]. Another designated [[DD-19]] served aboard the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Characteristics==
A model of [[Supervisor droid/Legends|supervisor droid]],<ref name="CSWE" /> the DD-19 "Overseer" labor pool [[Droid/Legends|droid]],<ref name="TNEGtG" /> more simply known as the DD-19 "Overseer" Droid<ref name="The DarkStryder Campaign" /> or DD-19 Overseer,<ref name="CSWE" /> was a bipedal droid with [[Color/Legends|gray]] plating, featuring two tracked [[Limb/Legends|feet]], a pair of extendable arms, and three black [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]]. Standing 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height, it was equipped with a [[Broadband antenna/Legends|broadband antenna-receiver]], and a [[Restraining bolt/Legends|restraining bolt]] applicator and remover.<ref name="The DarkStryder Campaign" />

The DD-19 Overseer was not only capable of processing the needs of several other droids at once, but could also communicate the necessary commands in the electronic information-dense [[Language/Legends|languages]] favored by many sophisticated droids. However, while the DD-19 Overseer was actually able to watch over up to fifty droids at once, field experience proved that a maximum of fifteen to twenty was more manageable.<ref name="The DarkStryder Campaign" />

==History==
Manufactured by [[Ubrikkian Steamworks]],<ref name="TNEGtG" /> the DD-19 Overseer was available for a price of 4,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="The DarkStryder Campaign" /> When the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] personally quashed the company's renewal contract with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Ubrikkian quietly retired its [[DD-13 medical assistant droid/Legends|DD-13 medical assistant droid]] to focus its attention on the more profitable DD-19 Overseer.<ref name="TNEGtG" />

In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], a DD-19 Overseer was infected by [[Iron Eclipse]], a [[Computer virus/Legends|technovirus]] that made them loyal to [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco]] and [[Iaclyn Stark]] and overrode any programming inhibiting them from harming [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings. When [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] agent [[Jahan Cross]] [[Mission to Eclipse|inflitrated]] [[Eclipse station (Reltooine)|Eclipse]],<ref name="Iron Eclipse 4" /> the [[Stark family]]'s half-completed [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] resort<ref name="Iron Eclipse 5">[[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 5|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 5]]</ref> over the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Reltooine]], the DD-19 Overseer was among the Iron Eclipse-infected droids that attacked [[Gender/Legends|him]] on the command of Iaclyn Stark.<ref name="Iron Eclipse 4" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="Dating">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]].</ref> during the [[DarkStryder Campaign]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a masculine DD-19 Overseer designated [[DD-19]] worked in the droid repair bay of the modified [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]''. Alongside his [[SE4 servant droid]] assistant, [[SE-4-17]], DD-19 performed much of the maintenance work there. DD-19 earned the nickname "the slavemaster" because of his seemingly harsh treatment of other droids, having the authority to apply restraining bolts if a droid became unruly.<ref name="The DarkStryder Campaign" />

==Behind the scenes==
The DD-19 "Overseer" labor pool droid was first mentioned as the DD-19 "Overseer" Droid in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a [[1995]] supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', where it was depicted in an illustration by [[Doug Shuler]].<ref name="The DarkStryder Campaign" /> The [[2006]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]], provided the name DD-19 "Overseer" labor pool droid,<ref name="TNEGtG" /> while the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' used the name DD-19 Overseer.<ref name="CSWE" /> The DD-19 Overseer first appeared in [[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 4|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 4]], a comic written by [[John Ostrander]] and pencilled by [[Stéphane Créty]]<ref name="Iron Eclipse 4" /> that was published on [[March 14]], [[2012]].<ref name="Iron Eclipse 4 release date">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54013/star_wars_agent_of_the_empire_-_iron_eclipse_2011_4|text=Star Wars: Agent of the Empire - Iron Eclipse (2011) #4}}</ref>
